items.name: The Chaotic Coder
items.description: Works on whatever feels urgent, no system, high stress, inconsistent output
items.symptoms: Always busy but rarely productive, frequent context switching, no routines
items.focus_areas: Time management basics (Eisenhower Matrix) + sleep hygiene
items.first_habit: Time block 2 hours of deep work tomorrow morning

items.name: The Efficient Worker
items.description: Has good time management but neglects wellness, eventually burns out
items.symptoms: High output but declining energy, chronic stress, neglecting health

items.name: The Balanced Professional
items.description: Maintains both productivity AND wellness, sustainable high performance
items.symptoms: Consistent output, good energy, healthy boundaries, continuous improvement
items.focus_areas: Optimization + advanced techniques (OKRs, deep work blocks)

items.name: The Productivity Master
items.description: Has refined their system over years, mentors others, consistently high performance
items.symptoms: Effortless productivity, strong boundaries, helping others improve
items.first_habit: Share your system with a junior developer

comparisons.insight: Where you are NOW isn't where you'll be in 6 months. Most developers move from Chaotic Coder to Efficient Worker in 4-8 weeks, then to Balanced Professional in 3-6 months. The key is starting with ONE habit and building from there.

keyInsight: Your diagnostic score tells you which archetype you are — not as a judgment, but as a starting point. Every master was once a chaotic coder. The difference is they started building one habit at a time.
